page: Amazon Web Services hpc6a.48xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server optimized for high-performance computing workloads. It features 96 dedicated physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processors running at 3.6 GHz, with one thread per core. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. It utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is supported by a 100 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, passmark CPU score, and memory score. The dedicated core configuration yields strong single-core performance and exceptional multi-threaded decompression capabilities. It also achieves top-tier results in software compilation, ray tracing, and cryptography tests. However, text generation for medium-sized language models is a relative bottleneck, performing in the bottom 25% of tested systems.

From a resource density perspective, the hpc6a.48xlarge offers high compute efficiency by omitting local storage and GPU hardware, allowing users to allocate budget strictly to raw CPU and memory bandwidth. The lack of hyperthreading ensures predictable performance for parallel computing tasks, though it may limit throughput for highly concurrent, lightweight web applications.
This instance is designed for compute-intensive workloads such as high-performance computing, scientific simulations, physics modeling, large-scale software compilation, and parallel data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services hpc6a.48xlarge is an x86_64 high-performance computing server powered by 96 dedicated physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The server does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, the hpc6a.48xlarge delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, software compilation speeds, and memory bandwidth, though it shows weaker performance in text generation for medium-sized language models. This instance is highly cost-efficient for raw parallel computing tasks due to its dedicated core design and lack of unneeded local storage overhead. It is best suited for high-performance computing, physics simulations, cryptography, and large-scale software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services hpc6a.48xlarge is a high-performance computing server featuring 96 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for parallel processing.

tagline: A dedicated 96-core AMD EPYC compute node with 100 Gbps networking, optimized for parallel scientific simulations and high-performance computing.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated physical AMD EPYC 7R13 cores running at 3.6 GHz
384.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 4.0 GB per core
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and compilation performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators to maximize compute cost efficiency
Optimized for high-performance computing, physics simulations, and cryptography
